title: Pairing, System Reasoning, Debugging, and Project Defense · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs pairing, system reasoning, debugging, and project defense from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-format technical defense packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
Reproduce the symptom, bound the last-known-good state, rank hypotheses, run discriminating checks, repair the cause, and retain regression evidence.
Elicit stakeholders, workload, quality scenarios, data, trust, failure, cost, operations, and evolution before proposing components.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Project Defense
steps.instruction:
review case — A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The review variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

Inspect the Project Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Project Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Project Defense, keep Project Defense connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Project Defense, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Project Defense, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Project Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Project Defense, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The review variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Project Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Speaking in “we” avoids the need to identify personal contribution.

stimulus.title: Project Defense case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The review variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Speaking in “we” avoids the need to identify personal contribution.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence. Artifact under review: multi-format technical defense packet.

options.text: The Project Defense record says multi-format technical defense packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Project Defense.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Project Defense without its stated preconditions, omit the multi-format technical defense packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Project Defense for multi-format technical defense packet.
The record names the assumption, applies Project Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
